title: coordinator/permission: always_allow HashSet lookup degraded to O(N) linear scan
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/coordinator/permission.rs:90-97 (is_always_allowed)
## Violation
```rust
pub fn is_always_allowed(&self, teammate: &TeammateId, tool_name: &str) -> bool {
    // The HashSet requires owned-key lookup; we keep the
    // lookup allocation-free by comparing inside a `iter().any`.
    self.always_allowed
        .iter()
        .any(|(t, tool)| t == teammate && tool == tool_name)
}
```

The HashSet is being walked linearly to dodge an owned-key allocation. The comment justifies the choice as "allocation-free", but for K cached pairs and Q queries the cost is O(K*Q) when a HashSet was supposed to give O(Q). Bash + write_file + edit_file + ... + 10 teammates = 70 pairs scanned per check. Hot path during dispatch.

## Mandated refactor
Either store `HashMap<TeammateId, HashSet<String>>` (one allocation on insert, O(1) on lookup with `String::as_str` comparison via `HashSet::contains` taking `&str`), or accept the one-time `tool_name.to_string()` allocation per check — modern allocators make this faster than a 70-element linear scan, regardless. Drop the misleading "allocation-free" comment.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
